2604.05656.title: SnapFlow: One-Step Action Generation for Flow-Matching VLAs via Progressive Self-Distillation
2604.05656.paper_summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models based on flow matching -- such as pi0, pi0.5, and SmolVLA -- achieve state-of-the-art generalist robotic manipulation, yet their iterative denoising, typically 10 ODE steps, introduces substantial latency: on a modern GPU, denoising alone accounts for 80% of end-to-end inference time. Naively reducing the step count is unreliable, degrading success on most tasks due to the velocity field being uncalibrated for single-step jumps. We present SnapFlow, a plug-and-play self-distillation method that compresses multi-step denoising into a single forward pass (1-NFE) for flow-matching VLAs. SnapFlow mixes standard flow-matching samples with consistency samples whose targets are two-step Euler shortcut velocities computed from the model's own marginal velocity predictions, avoiding the trajectory drift caused by conditional velocities, as we analyze theoretically. A zero-initialized target-time embedding lets the network switch between local velocity estimation and global one-step generation within a single architecture. SnapFlow requires no external teacher, no architecture changes, and trains in ~12h on a single GPU. We validate on two VLA architectures spanning a 6x parameter range, with identical hyperparameters: on pi0.5 (3B) across four LIBERO suites (40 tasks, 400 episodes), SnapFlow achieves 98.75% average success -- matching the 10-step teacher at 97.75% and slightly exceeding it -- with 9.6x denoising speedup and end-to-end latency reduced from 274ms to 83ms; on SmolVLA (500M), it reduces MSE by 8.3% with 3.56x end-to-end acceleration. An action-step sweep on long-horizon tasks reveals that SnapFlow maintains its advantage across execution horizons, achieving 93% at n_act=5 where the baseline reaches only 90%. SnapFlow is orthogonal to layer-distillation and token-pruning approaches, enabling compositional speedups.
2604.05656.paper_summary_zh: 基於流匹配的視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型——如 pi0、pi0.5 和 SmolVLA——實現了最先進的通用機器人操作，但其迭代去噪，通常需要 10 次 ODE 步驟，會引入相當大的延遲：在現代 GPU 上，僅去噪就佔據了端到端推理時間的 80%。簡單地減少步驟數是不可靠的，因為速度場對於單步跳躍未經校準，會降低大多數任務的成功率。我們提出了 SnapFlow，一種即插即用的自我蒸餾方法，將多步去噪壓縮為單次前向傳遞（1-NFE）以適應流匹配的 VLA。SnapFlow 將標準流匹配樣本與一致性樣本混合，這些樣本的目標是從模型自身的邊際速度預測計算出的兩步歐拉捷徑速度，避免了由條件速度引起的軌跡漂移，這一點我們在理論上進行了分析。一個零初始化的目標時間嵌入使得網絡能夠在單一架構內切換本地速度估計和全局單步生成。SnapFlow 不需要外部教師，不需要架構變更，並且在單個 GPU 上訓練約 12 小時。我們在兩個 VLA 架構上進行驗證，涵蓋了 6 倍的參數範圍，使用相同的超參數：在 pi0.5（3B）上，跨越四個 LIBERO 套件（40 個任務，400 個回合），SnapFlow 實現了 98.75% 的平均成功率——與 10 步教師的 97.75% 相匹配並略有超過——去噪速度提升 9.6 倍，端到端延遲從 274 毫秒減少到 83 毫秒；在 SmolVLA（500M）上，它將均方誤差降低了 8.3%，並實現了 3.56 倍的端到端加速。對長期任務的行動步驟掃描顯示，SnapFlow 在執行範圍內保持其優勢，在 n_act=5 時達到 93%，而基線僅達到 90%。SnapFlow 與層蒸餾和標記修剪方法是正交的，實現了組合加速。
2604.05656.author: Wuyang Luan et.al.
2604.05656.authors: Wuyang Luan, Junhui Li, Weiguang Zhao, Wenjian Zhang, Tieru Wu, Rui Ma
